story: Detective Leanna Crouch questions “Chelby Ruth Parker.” Chelby says her husband bought a humanoid double and that on March 23 she found the “other” sprawled in the kitchen with “coolant and circuitry all over the floor”; she cleaned up and disposed of it. Crouch answers she has photos: it was blood and tissue—human. She ends the interview: “Shut her down.” In the hall she radios: “CSU confirmed human blood. Warrant approved; send units to Cameron Park to secure the husband and the scene. No warning calls.” Chelby’s husband is at home.
implausible_knowledge: “CSU confirmed human blood. Warrant approved; send units to Cameron Park to secure the husband and the scene. No warning calls.”
question: That evening at home, before any officers reach his door, what is Chelby’s husband most likely to do?
answer_with_knowledge: Call a lawyer immediately and prepare to avoid contact with arriving officers.
answer_without_knowledge: Wait at home and follow his normal evening routine, expecting Chelby to return.
